title: ISCRAM 2026 - Building Stronger Futures. Ensuring Public Safety in Times of Crisis
content: The theme of ISCRAM 2026, ‘Building Stronger Futures’, highlights the collective risks posed by natural hazards, cybersecurity, artificial intelligence, critical infrastructure, and climate change.
snippet: The theme of ISCRAM 2026, ‘Building Stronger Futures’, highlights the collective risks posed by natural hazards, cybersecurity, artificial intelligence, critical infrastructure, and climate change.

title: DRRP Newsletter Issue #2: Disasters, impacts and response in OACPS countries
content: This second edition of the newsletter focuses on Hurricane Melissa in the Caribbean islands.
snippet: This second edition of the newsletter focuses on Hurricane Melissa in the Caribbean islands.

title: Intra Africa, Caribbean and Pacific (ACP) Disaster Risk Reduction Programme (DRRP)
content: The Intra-ACP Disaster Risk Reduction (DRR) Programme represents one of the European Union’s key cooperation instruments with the Organisation of African, Caribbean and Pacific States (OACPS) in strengthening risk management and resilience to disasters.
snippet: The Intra-ACP Disaster Risk Reduction (DRR) Programme represents one of the European Union’s key cooperation instruments with the Organisation of African, Caribbean and Pacific States (OACPS) in stren

title: Can music improve tsunami preparedness? Lessons from Timor-Leste
content: Tsunami risk is already well known in many coastal communities, but translating awareness into sustained engagement and action remains a challenge. For World Tsunami Awareness Day 2025, agencies explored whether music could help bridge that gap.

title: A next-generation telescope for space weather research
content: China’s new dual‑band interplanetary scintillation telescope uses a phased‑array feed and AI calibration to deliver unprecedented sensitivity for tracking solar wind disturbances, strengthening global space‑weather monitoring and forecasting.
snippet: China’s new dual‑band interplanetary scintillation telescope uses a phased‑array feed and AI calibration to deliver unprecedented sensitivity for tracking solar wind disturbances, strengthening global

title: End-to-end mapping solution for volcano evacuation routes - Bali, Indonesia
content: The End-to-end mapping solution strengthens strengthens local capacity in Bali to map evacuation routes and improve volcanic disaster preparedness around Mount Agung.
snippet: The End-to-end mapping solution strengthens strengthens local capacity in Bali to map evacuation routes and improve volcanic disaster preparedness around Mount Agung.

title: State of the climate Kenya
content: The State of the Climate Kenya 2025 report shows how rising temperatures and uneven rainfall are already affecting Kenya’s agriculture, water, health, and livelihoods, while warning of higher climate risks in 2026.
snippet: The State of the Climate Kenya 2025 report shows how rising temperatures and uneven rainfall are already affecting Kenya’s agriculture, water, health, and livelihoods, while warning of higher climate

title: Tech-equipped Indigenous firefighters protect Thai forests
content: In the dry-season heat of northern Thailand, Hmong villagers zip through forested slopes, blasting tinder with leaf blowers and cutting through brush with machetes, while others scan for smoke on live feeds from their phones.
snippet: In the dry-season heat of northern Thailand, Hmong villagers zip through forested slopes, blasting tinder with leaf blowers and cutting through brush with machetes, while others scan for smoke on live

title: Optimization of municipal bridge retrofitting based on nationwide spatial clustering integrating seismic risk and fiscal capacity in Japan
content: This article presents a nationwide framework for prioritizing seismic retrofitting of municipally managed bridges in Japan by combining bridge-level seismic risk, municipal fiscal capacity, spatial clustering of recovery capacity.
snippet: This article presents a nationwide framework for prioritizing seismic retrofitting of municipally managed bridges in Japan by combining bridge-level seismic risk, municipal fiscal capacity, spatial cl

title: Assessing flood resilience in West Virginia communities using socioeconomic and physical vulnerability indicators: implications for sustainable planning
content: This study assesses flood resilience in West Virginia by combining socioeconomic and physical vulnerability indicators, showing that the most at-risk communities are those where social disadvantage and high flood exposure overlap.

title: Study links past emissions to trillions in future economic damages
content: Future climate damages from past greenhouse gas emissions dwarf the economic harm already inflicted.
snippet: Future climate damages from past greenhouse gas emissions dwarf the economic harm already inflicted.

title: Seychelles country climate and development report
content: The report outlines how Seychelles can achieve sustainable economic growth while strengthening climate resilience in the face of increasing environmental risks.
snippet: The report outlines how Seychelles can achieve sustainable economic growth while strengthening climate resilience in the face of increasing environmental risks.

title: Social influences on behavioural response to earthquake shaking: evidence from CCTV footage
content: This study examines how people respond to earthquake shaking, focusing on the role of social influence in shaping behaviour.
snippet: This study examines how people respond to earthquake shaking, focusing on the role of social influence in shaping behaviour.

title: Urban flood and gully erosion risk management in the Congo basin
content: Early warning systems: Rapid assessments from four partner countries: Democratic Republic of the Congo, Mali, Niger, and South Sudan
snippet: Early warning systems: Rapid assessments from four partner countries: Democratic Republic of the Congo, Mali, Niger, and South Sudan

title: Indonesia’s economic security requires ecological resilience
content: Indonesia’s economic growth is increasingly threatened by climate disasters, highlighting the urgent need to integrate environmental protection and ecological resilience into national economic and policy planning.
snippet: Indonesia’s economic growth is increasingly threatened by climate disasters, highlighting the urgent need to integrate environmental protection and ecological resilience into national economic and pol

title: Cascading impacts of natural disasters in a connected world
content: Extreme climate-related disasters create interconnected cascading impacts across sectors and regions, making it essential to improve global risk modelling and management systems.
snippet: Extreme climate-related disasters create interconnected cascading impacts across sectors and regions, making it essential to improve global risk modelling and management systems.

title: The Papua New Guinea national disaster risk reduction framework (2017–2030)
content: The Papua New Guinea National Disaster Risk Reduction Framework (2017–2030) outlines the country’s long-term strategy to reduce disaster risk and strengthen resilience.
snippet: The Papua New Guinea National Disaster Risk Reduction Framework (2017–2030) outlines the country’s long-term strategy to reduce disaster risk and strengthen resilience.

title: Urban heat, mortality, and economic costs: Evidence from Bangkok, Thailand
content: This study aims to provide an operational basis for urban planning and investment decisions by quantifying heat-induced health burdens across Bangkok’s 50 amphurs (districts) and the associated citywide economic costs.
snippet: This study aims to provide an operational basis for urban planning and investment decisions by quantifying heat-induced health burdens across Bangkok’s 50 amphurs (districts) and the associated citywi

title: The inter-regional economic impact of the reduction in tourism following the Maui wildfires
content: This analysis examines the wider economic effects of the Maui wildfires by quantifying the statewide reduction in tourism spending in 2023 and 2024.
snippet: This analysis examines the wider economic effects of the Maui wildfires by quantifying the statewide reduction in tourism spending in 2023 and 2024.

title: Rwanda national disaster risk reduction and management policy
content: The Rwanda National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Policy (2023) sets out a national framework to reduce disaster risks and strengthen resilience.
snippet: The Rwanda National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Policy (2023) sets out a national framework to reduce disaster risks and strengthen resilience.

title: National disaster management office strategic plan 2016 - 2020
content: The National Disaster Management Office (NDMO) Strategic Plan (2016–2020) outlines Vanuatu’s approach to strengthening disaster risk management in one of the world’s most hazard-exposed countries.
snippet: The National Disaster Management Office (NDMO) Strategic Plan (2016–2020) outlines Vanuatu’s approach to strengthening disaster risk management in one of the world’s most hazard-exposed countries.

title: Thailand national adaptation plan
content: Thailand’s National Adaptation Plan (2023) provides a comprehensive framework to strengthen climate resilience by reducing vulnerability and integrating adaptation into national, sectoral, and local development planning.

title: Sierra Leone national disaster risk management policy
content: The revised National Disaster Risk Management Policy (NDRMP) for Sierra Leone is a critical response to the escalating frequency and intensity of disasters, which are increasingly exacerbated by climate change.
snippet: The revised National Disaster Risk Management Policy (NDRMP) for Sierra Leone is a critical response to the escalating frequency and intensity of disasters, which are increasingly exacerbated by clima

title: National adaptation plan for St. Vincent and the Grenadines
content: The National Adaptation Plan (2019) for Saint Vincent and the Grenadines sets out a long-term framework (2018–2030) to reduce climate vulnerability and embed climate change adaptation into national development planning.

title: Early Warning and Early Action in Least Developed Countries in Conflict and Post-Conflict Situations in Africa
content: Building upon the workshop on “EWEA in LDCs in Conflict and Post-conflict Situations in Africa”, held in December 2025 in Nairobi, Kenya, this webinar will share key findings, advance discussion on practical solutions, and support follow-up actions.

title: Canada: Jasper fires 2024
content: This collection brings together key articles examining the 2024 wildfire season in Canada, a year marked by widespread fires, significant environmental impacts, and growing concern over the country’s evolving fire regime.
snippet: This collection brings together key articles examining the 2024 wildfire season in Canada, a year marked by widespread fires, significant environmental impacts, and growing concern over the country’s

title: 5 things to know about the links between climate change, migration and health
content: Climate change, migration and health are tightly connected: climate impacts shape mobility, and both moving and being unable to move create physical and mental health risks.
snippet: Climate change, migration and health are tightly connected: climate impacts shape mobility, and both moving and being unable to move create physical and mental health risks.

title: One year on: from recovery to resilience after Tropical Cyclone Alfred
content: TC Alfred was the first tropical cyclone to impact the Brisbane region in more than 50 years. It made landfall as a category 1 but peaked as a category 4 off the coast and ranked among 2025’s costliest global disasters.
snippet: TC Alfred was the first tropical cyclone to impact the Brisbane region in more than 50 years. It made landfall as a category 1 but peaked as a category 4 off the coast and ranked among 2025’s costlies

title: Storm ready: NOAA’s new mobile radar fleet bridges gap between research and weather safety
content: Three cutting-edge mobile weather radars unveiled by NOAA will allow researchers to deploy research-grade technology to the front lines of tornadoes, wildfires, hail storms, flash flooding and severe wind events,

title: National Consultant – Development of Localization Model – Sri Lanka
content: The consultancy will focus on documentation, analytical synthesis, and conceptual modeling based on existing experience and stakeholder reflection, rather than conducting an institutional assessment.
snippet: The consultancy will focus on documentation, analytical synthesis, and conceptual modeling based on existing experience and stakeholder reflection, rather than conducting an institutional assessment.

title: How disaster risk financing can anchor Africa's development and strengthen its resilience
content: Africa’s rising climate shocks are eroding development gains. The article argues that disaster risk financing — especially ARC’s parametric insurance — helps governments respond faster, protect vulnerable communities and safeguard long‑term growth.
snippet: Africa’s rising climate shocks are eroding development gains. The article argues that disaster risk financing — especially ARC’s parametric insurance — helps governments respond faster, protect vulner

title: National Consultant – Development of Localization Model (Nepal)
content: ADPC is implementing Phase 3 of the Asian Preparedness Partnership (APP), a multi-stakeholder localization model launched in 2016 in response to the World Humanitarian Summit’s call to shift power to local actors in disaster preparedness and response.
snippet: ADPC is implementing Phase 3 of the Asian Preparedness Partnership (APP), a multi-stakeholder localization model launched in 2016 in response to the World Humanitarian Summit’s call to shift power to

title: Chopping down areas of tropical rainforest is causing rising temperatures linked to thousands of deaths
content: Tropical deforestation is rapidly amplifying local heat, exposing over 300 million people to higher temperatures and contributing to an estimated 28,000 heat‑related deaths each year.
snippet: Tropical deforestation is rapidly amplifying local heat, exposing over 300 million people to higher temperatures and contributing to an estimated 28,000 heat‑related deaths each year.

title: Journal of Social Welfare and Management
content: The Journal of Social Welfare Management addresses the pressing global social welfare issues emerging from demographic shifts, migration patterns, and economic globalization.
snippet: The Journal of Social Welfare Management addresses the pressing global social welfare issues emerging from demographic shifts, migration patterns, and economic globalization.

title: Climate Dynamics (Springer)
content: Climate Dynamics is an international journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research on all aspects of the dynamics of the global climate system.
snippet: Climate Dynamics is an international journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research on all aspects of the dynamics of the global climate system.

title: Tripartite agreement programme – Building urban flood risk resilience in Nigeria
content: This case study explains how a public–private partnership in Nigeria is helping the government better manage flood risks, especially in Lagos.
snippet: This case study explains how a public–private partnership in Nigeria is helping the government better manage flood risks, especially in Lagos.

title: Design of a virtual test bed for the Italian territory: Proof of concept for multi-hazard scenarios effects in the context of a national resilience assessment
content: This study presents a proof‑of‑concept virtual test bed designed to model multi‑hazard scenarios and support national resilience assessment across Italy.
snippet: This study presents a proof‑of‑concept virtual test bed designed to model multi‑hazard scenarios and support national resilience assessment across Italy.

title: From flood risk to resilience: Practical flood adaptation strategies for refugee settlements in Ethiopia and South Sudan
content: This report outlines practical, evidence-based flood risk reduction strategies for two displacement settings in East Africa: Kule Refugee Camp in Ethiopia and Renk Transit Centre in South Sudan.
snippet: This report outlines practical, evidence-based flood risk reduction strategies for two displacement settings in East Africa: Kule Refugee Camp in Ethiopia and Renk Transit Centre in South Sudan.

title: Online Workshop on Strengthening Whole-of-Society Capacities for Disaster Risk Reduction and Climate Resilience: Inclusive Governance and Innovative Mechanisms
content: The workshop will introduce concepts, frameworks, practical approaches to strengthen inclusive DRR, climate resilience, and climate action through effective governance, risk-informed planning, private sector engagement, and technology-enabled solutions.

title: Intra Africa, Caribbean and Pacific (ACP) Disaster Risk Reduction Programme
content: This web-based collection shares local and regional experiences for the benefit of the wider DRR community and OACP countries, produced in the context of the Intra-ACP Disaster Risk Reduction (DRR) Programme.

title: Extreme heat risk perceptions and readiness in schools
content: This study outlines how rising extreme heat poses growing risks to pupils and staff in schools across the United Kingdom, highlighting limited preparedness and the absence of robust heat action plans.
snippet: This study outlines how rising extreme heat poses growing risks to pupils and staff in schools across the United Kingdom, highlighting limited preparedness and the absence of robust heat action plans.

title: Extreme heat deaths must be avoided
content: Extreme heat deaths in India are predictable and preventable. Early warnings, community action and stronger governance can protect vulnerable workers, reduce inequality‑driven exposure, and ensure heat‑related deaths are properly counted and addressed.
